mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real GDP growth indicates a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, aggressive Fed tightening compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPI exceeds the threshold and the Fed raises rates, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day forward return median remains below zero when PAYEMS shows consecutive monthly gains, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.0 over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.0 million units signals weakening consumer confidence and credit conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when housing starts breach the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When rising unemployment coincides with declining retail sales, it confirms a demand-driven recessionary shock that severely pressures equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in directional accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the interaction coefficient loses statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress where industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth, inventory drawdowns force firms to raise prices, temporarily boosting equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or statistically insignificant when industrial output outpaces GDP growth, or the signal's predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.
